response:
# Whispers of the Sea

The ocean breathes in tides of blue,
A rhythm old, yet ever new.
Waves embrace the sandy shore,
Then slip away to rise once more.

Salt-kissed air and seabirds' cries,
Horizons stretched beneath vast skies.
Secrets drift in depths below,
Where sunlight fades and currents flow.

She holds both calm and tempest wild—
At once a mother, once a child.
Forever dancing, never still,
The sea bends to no mortal will.
